completed_e3_candidates.interpretation: This candidate has crossed the E3 threshold. The CHGNet force proxy and QE metallic response are consistent with TiN being a robust conventional-metal benchmark-adjacent candidate. This is not a novelty breakthrough candidate, but it is a credible queue anchor for the exploratory-metal branch.

This candidate has crossed the E3 threshold as a computed DFT-supported candidate because the full bounded `relax -> scf -> nscf -> dos` chain now exists and is verified. The present fixed-cell AlB2-type proxy does not support a finite-DOS metallic interpretation at `E_F`. No Tc claim is justified here; the next evidence-bearing step is cell/prototype review and, if warranted, a SOC-aware or alternate-structure follow-up.

This candidate has crossed the E3 threshold as a computed DFT-supported candidate. The current fixed-cell AlB2-type setup supports a finite-DOS metallic interpretation, which is stronger than the earlier queue-promotion-only status. No Tc claim is justified here. The `vc-relax` follow-up and bounded EOS check do not erase the fixed-cell E3 result, but together they block immediate phonon promotion on the present input deck. The next evidence-bearing step should be a pseudopotential / lattice-parameter audit or alternate verified NbB2 setup, not direct phonon promotion.

This candidate has crossed the E3 threshold as a computed DFT-supported candidate because the full bounded `relax -> scf -> nscf -> dos` chain now exists and is verified. The present fixed-cell AlB2-type proxy does not support a finite-DOS metallic interpretation at `E_F`. No Tc claim is justified here; the next evidence-bearing step is cell/prototype review before any phonon-style promotion.

This candidate has crossed the E3 threshold as a computed DFT-supported candidate because the bounded fixed-cell `relax -> scf -> nscf -> dos` chain is complete and verified. The present AlB2-type proxy does not support a finite-DOS metallic interpretation at `E_F`. No Tc claim is justified here; the next evidence-bearing step is cell/prototype review rather than phonon promotion.

The run completed successfully and therefore reaches E3 as a computed candidate. However, the present AlB2-type MoB2 setup does not yet support a strong metallicity claim. This candidate should stay in the queue, but under review rather than direct promotion.

loop_assessment.current_focus: advance evidence quality over candidate volume
next_round_priority: Force the loop to expire stale anchors instead of allowing indefinite prescreen refresh
Prioritize mainline charge-transfer candidates when they are promotion-ready and gate-eligible
Increase dossier evidence density for E3 candidates before promoting phonon work
Record failures and review-needed cases before retrying alternate prototypes
autonomy_gaps: external public leaderboard publishing remains less stable than local state
maker-checker separation is stronger in code flow than in scientific claims
high-severity stalls still depend on bounded local recovery actions rather than a broader orchestration layer
